message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should return premium 65 G for 3 runes — 60 G base (block of 3 alike) + 5 G fee"
Expected behavior: Exactly 3 runes → special block premium 60 G instead of 3 × 25 = 75 G
Current error: Expected 65, received 80 (3 × 25 + 5 = 80)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Write the minimal code to make this test pass. The spec says "a building block of 3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G." The block requires exactly 3 of the same component type.

Components are runes and moonstones (25 G each). Main items are sword/amulet/staff/potion.

The minimal approach: after computing individual item premiums, check if there are exactly 3 components of the same type and if so, use 60 G instead of 75 G for that group.

Keep all 6 existing tests passing too.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll start by reading the test file and implementation file to understand the current state.
Now let me run the tests to see the current state and confirm the failure.

input.description: Run tests to see current state

content.text:
Good. I can see the failing test: 3 runes produces 80 (3 x 25 + 5) but should produce 65 (60 block price + 5 fee). The current implementation simply sums individual base premiums. I need to add minimal logic to detect when there are exactly 3 components of the same type and apply the 60 G block price instead.

The minimal approach: after computing the sum of individual premiums, count components by type, and if any type has exactly 3, subtract the difference (75 - 60 = 15 G).

Let me implement the minimal change.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass (including the 6 previously passing ones and the new one).

Let me verify the final state of the implementation file.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added block-of-3-alike discount logic. After computing the sum of individual base premiums, the code counts items by type. If any type has exactly 3 items, it replaces the individual pricing (3 x unit price) with the special block price of 60 G.

**Result**: All tests now pass (7 passing, 35 todo).

**Approach**: This is minimal because:
- It only handles the `=== 3` case that the current test demands (no generalization to other block sizes).
- It uses the hardcoded block price of 60 G directly as specified.
- It counts by type so it correctly applies the "alike" (same component type) requirement.
- No refactoring of existing code was done -- the original reduce logic is preserved and the block discount is applied as a post-processing adjustment.

**Files modified**:
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.ts`

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
